fields.description:
## Why
Automate the FFO rebase-to-tip-then-submit so parallel agent sessions stop hand-rolling it and racing; serialize so the tested tree is always the merged tree with no thrash.

## What
A project service (Python, project infra — NOT `src/rebar` core) that, for the front `Autosubmit`+submittable change/chain: rebases to the current tip, awaits a fresh `Verified +1`, and ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA. Holds a single in-flight `wipChain`.

**Poll cadence (design-fixed).** The loop runs on a fixed **15 s poll interval** — the "bot poll interval" that S4 and S5 reference. Rationale: a freshly-`Autosubmit`'d change is picked up within ~15 s (good agent UX) while staying gentle on the Gerrit REST API; it is intentionally distinct from `land`'s own 30 s client-side poll (S4). The bot writes its liveness heartbeat once per poll iteration (S5 AC5), so under normal operation the heartbeat is never older than ~15 s.

**Terminology — `submittable` under FFO.** Under Fast-Forward-Only `main` (ADR-0040), Gerrit's `submittable` field **already requires the change to be a descendant of the current `main` tip**: a behind-tip change is reported **non-submittable** (`submittable=false`), and `submittable=True` means both gate votes are at MAX *and* the change sits on the current tip. Two consequences this loop must respect: (1) **TOCTOU** — `submittable=True` is only true as of the read; `main` can advance before `POST /submit` and flip the change to non-submittable, so re-confirm submittability immediately before submitting, and if a stale submit is nonetheless attempted FFO refuses it with a `not fast-forward` error. (2) A native `submittable=false` does **not** say *why* (behind-tip vs a failing gate vote) — the distinction S4 needs for `needs_rebase`.

## Dependencies
Depends on **S1** (`sterilized-papery-horse`, 843b-801f-9ef9-442d) — the `Autosubmit` label + its **requester-votable ACL** (the vote is granted to group `Contributors`, so an *agent* sets the label under its OWN identity via S4's `land`; this loop casts no votes of its own). The loop only *selects on* `label:Autosubmit+1`, so the label and its Contributor-votable ACL must exist and be live before the loop can select or drive anything.

## Scope
Happy-path loop + serialization + identity/attribution. Failure paths, deploy, and the agent-facing command are separate stories.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Selects the front `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` change/chain; **FIFO ordering keyed on the `Autosubmit` vote's timestamp** (oldest-voted-submittable-first). (`is:submittable` is the point-in-time candidate filter; the change is only *landed* after the pre-submit re-confirmation — see the TOCTOU guard below.)
- [ ] **Rebase-selection rule:** detect a relation chain via `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/related` (the RelatedChanges endpoint) — a chain of >1 non-merge member rebases with `POST /rebase:chain`. A **`--no-ff` merge change is ALWAYS rebased with `POST /changes/{id}/rebase`** (first-parent-only → `MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE`), **NEVER `/rebase:chain`**, even when it sits at a chain tip — `/rebase:chain` is for non-merge chains only. A single non-merge change also uses `POST /changes/{id}/rebase`. `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` preserves author/DCO `Signed-off-by`/`rebar-ticket` trailer.
- [ ] Awaits a **fresh** `Verified +1` — defined as a `Verified +1` on the exact rebased patchset SHA (not a copied/carried vote from a prior patchset) — before submit (`LLM-Review` carries `TRIVIAL_REBASE`/`MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` per ADR-0041; `Verified` re-runs on the new patchset).
- [ ] Ancestor-atomic submit of the exact tested SHA. **FFO TOCTOU guard + re-drive:** `submittable=True` is only true as of the read — `main` can advance before `POST /submit`, and under FFO that flips the change to non-submittable (ADR-0040). So immediately before `POST /submit`, re-confirm the tested SHA is still submittable (still a descendant of `main`'s current tip); if it is no longer submittable (main advanced) or the SHA changed, do NOT submit. If a stale submit is nonetheless attempted, treat the `not fast-forward` refusal identically. In either case **re-drive the same `wipChain`**: reset its `phase` to `rebasing`, rebase-to-new-tip → await fresh Verified → re-check, looping **at most `N=5` times**; after `N=5` re-drives park the change and surface `needs_rebase` rather than casting a stale submit.
- [ ] **`wipChain` schema:** a single in-memory record `{change_id, chain_member_ids[], tested_sha, verified_at, phase}` held by the single instance, where **`phase ∈ {selecting, rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting, idle}`**; `tested_sha` is set to the post-rebase patchset SHA on entering `awaiting_verified` and cleared on `idle`.
- [ ] Single `wipChain` / single instance (serialized) — never rebases multiple changes onto the same HEAD concurrently.
- [ ] Reads existing `Verified` votes only; casts NO votes (no gate-semantics change in Phase 1).
